idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas all classify a SINGLE gap (fade an extreme down-gap, mid-sigma SNR gap, gap-vs-close rejection, up-gap hysteresis). This strategy never looks at one gap: it accumulates a rolling FLUX of gaps and normalizes it by the standard deviation of the gaps themselves (a gap-dispersion 'capacity'), yielding a Mach-number z-score. The volatility primitive is the std of overnight gaps specifically, not bar ATR or close-to-close vol. The exit is a symmetric mirror stop-and-reverse with a deadband hold band, not a profit target, fixed-bar, or trailing stop.
idea.failure_mode: SPY overnight gaps carry a persistent small positive drift (the equity risk premium is concentrated overnight), so the cumulative flux is structurally biased positive. The short threshold may rarely trigger, or trigger only near tops, making the nominally symmetric system effectively long-biased with poorly-timed, infrequent shorts that bleed during the rare bear regimes.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade 3-day return direction only when daily high-low range shows strong positive lag-1 autocorrelation (coherent regime).
idea.hypothesis: The lag-1 autocorrelation of the daily high-low range measures regime coherence: when range clustering is strong, volatility is organized and directional moves carry low noise, so recent price drift tends to persist. When range autocorrelation is weak, bar-to-bar dynamics are choppy and directional bets are mostly noise. By using range autocorrelation purely as a tradability gate, the strategy only takes directional exposure inside coherent, high-signal-to-noise windows.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea uses the autocorrelation of the high-low RANGE series itself as a regime/SNR gate. The leverage-effect entry correlates returns with vol changes; range ideas in the list trade range shocks, compression coils, or deformation recoil as direct entry triggers. Here the range autocorrelation is neither a shock nor a coil nor an entry primitive - it is a coherence filter that switches a plain recent-return follow on or off. Different mechanism: persistence-of-volatility-clustering as a confidence switch, not a price pattern.
idea.failure_mode: Daily high-low range for an equity index is almost always positively autocorrelated (volatility clustering is a near-permanent feature), so the gate may rarely deactivate. If the threshold is essentially always satisfied, the strategy degenerates into a vanilla 3-day momentum follow and the autocorrelation filter contributes no real discriminating edge.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Enter long when consecutive sub-median-range bars build a compression streak past a threshold; rolling-high ATR trailing exit
idea.hypothesis: In equity indices, a run of consecutive bars whose high-low range stays below its own rolling median signals progressively coiling volatility - an 'infected' population of suppressed bars accumulating latent energy. The longer the unbroken compression streak, the more likely the next expansion resolves upward (the index's structural drift), so the streak length itself - not its depth - is the entry trigger. The release is then ridden with a ratcheting trailing stop until the expansion exhausts.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior list entries that touch compression use a coil PLUS a separate confirmation (z-score reclaim, calendar window, volume), or measure compression depth/percentile. Here the trigger is purely the COUNT of consecutive compressed bars crossing a threshold - the consecutive-streak primitive applied directly to the range-compression state machine, with no second indicator. No listed idea counts an unbroken run of sub-median-range bars as the standalone entry.
idea.failure_mode: Compression resolves direction-agnostically: a long streak of quiet bars can break downward just as easily as upward. The strategy assumes upward resolution and will repeatedly buy into volatility expansions that gap or trend down, where the trailing stop only locks in losses after the move has already gone against it.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Long SPY at the turn of the overnight-gap tide: second-derivative zero-cross of the cumulative-gap index while velocity is still negative
idea.hypothesis: Overnight gaps accumulate into a slow 'tidal' flow of cross-session positioning pressure. When that cumulative flow is still receding (negative velocity) but its acceleration freshly crosses up through zero, the overnight pull is decelerating its decline and about to reverse - a 'slack water before flood' moment. Entering at that turn captures the start of a one-to-two-week overnight-driven up-leg before it is obvious in price.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap items use gap LEVEL, gap direction-vs-intraday conflict, normalized cumulative-gap as a Mach number, or first-difference gap deceleration. None use the SECOND derivative (rate-of-change acceleration) of the cumulative overnight-gap index, and none gate entry on a zero-cross of that acceleration while velocity is still negative. The mechanism is a turn-of-tide detector on the curvature of an overnight-only equity curve, long-only with a profit-target/time-stop exit - structurally distinct from the Mach-number stop-and-reverse and from the seasonal-acceleration agreement strategy.
idea.failure_mode: On SPY daily data overnight gaps are small and largely noise; the cumulative-gap index drifts gently and its acceleration crosses zero frequently with little curvature signal. Those zero-crossings may have no real relationship to subsequent two-week returns, making entries effectively random and leaving the edge smaller than commission plus slippage.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade fresh distance-from-MA z-score breakouts only while a recent-extension count queue stays under capacity (uncrowded momentum), regime-gated by 200-day MA, fixed-bar exit
idea.hypothesis: When price first extends meaningfully away from its moving average (positive z-score) momentum tends to persist for several days, but trends that have ALREADY spent many recent bars in the extended state are crowded and late, and continuation from them is unreliable. By treating recent above-threshold bars as inflow into a fixed-capacity queue and refusing entries once that queue is full, the strategy harvests only the early, uncrowded segment of each move where the edge is concentrated.
idea.novelty_justification: The already-generated list contains a leaky-bucket queue that TRIGGERS on overflow and a spike-magnitude lockout. This strategy inverts both: a count-based crowding queue that SUPPRESSES entries when full, using capacity as a staleness/overcrowding filter rather than a trigger. No prior idea gates momentum by how long the move has already been extended, nor sizes positions by remaining queue headroom. It is a crowding-limited z-score breakout, not a streak, percentile, t-stat, or vol-deficit mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: The crowding cap may systematically discard exactly the strongest, most persistent trends (which by definition stay extended for many bars) and retain only weak early breakouts that fizzle out — i.e. it could select for false starts and reject the few moves that actually pay.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade live gaps only when the weekday's climatological gap mean agrees in direction; rolling-high ATR trailing exit
idea.hypothesis: Overnight order flow into SPY is not uniform across the trading week - settlement cycles, fund inflow timing, and macro-release scheduling give each weekday a persistent small directional gap bias. A live gap that points the same way as its own weekday's historical gap climatology reflects a structural overnight pressure rather than noise, and tends to extend over the following 1-2 sessions. An off-season gap (live gap opposing the weekday's typical bias) lacks that tailwind and is faded by the market.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior weekday item uses close-to-close return t-statistics for sizing; gap items in the list use intraday close location, return z-scores, drawdown extremes, or cumulative gap flux. This strategy is the only one that builds a per-weekday rolling climatology OF THE GAP ITSELF and uses it as the second leg of an AND-gate against the live gap event. The mechanism is event (today's gap z-score) cross-checked against calendar climatology (this weekday's mean gap) - neither a seasonal-return entry nor a gap-continuation rule alone.
idea.failure_mode: Weekday gap seasonality in SPY is weak and non-stationary. The weekday gap mean is estimated from only ~16 same-weekday samples, so the seasonal gate is mostly sampling noise; the AND can collapse into 'just trade large gaps,' leaving no edge after 7bps round-trip costs.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Range-weighted intrabar close-location oscillator with symmetric ±threshold hysteresis; hold long until mirror flip.
idea.hypothesis: Where SPY closes within each bar's high-low range encodes net buying/selling pressure, and wide-range bars carry more informative pressure than quiet ones. Averaging intrabar close-location with each bar weighted by its own range produces a bounded oscillator whose persistence above zero reflects an active accumulation regime. Entering when this range-weighted pressure is decisively positive and holding until it becomes decisively negative captures multi-day continuation while the symmetric band filters noise.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior ideas using intrabar close location either fade single range-expansion shocks (#3) or trade velocity/acceleration of mid-range position with a trailing stop (#22). This strategy is neither: it builds a continuous oscillator by averaging close location with each bar's contribution weighted by that bar's high-low range, so range dynamics directly determine which bars dominate the signal. Entry and exit are a single symmetric Schmitt-trigger crossing (+T enter, -T exit) — a hysteresis state machine, not a stop, percentile rank, or channel-position breakout used elsewhere.
idea.failure_mode: The symmetric hysteresis band means a strong uptrend can keep pressure positive far longer than the 1-2 day target horizon, turning the strategy into a slow trend follower and exposing it to deep give-back when pressure finally collapses through -T after a top.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when the drawdown series itself prints a fresh N-bar high while still negative, inside a recoverable-depth capacity band, size scaled by escaped depth
idea.hypothesis: When SPY is below its rolling peak, the drawdown ratio (close/peak-1) is itself a tradable series; a fresh multi-week high in that series while it is still negative marks the decisive turn of a recoverable dip before price reclaims the old peak. Such turns, when the dip was deep enough to matter but not so deep it signals a regime breakdown, tend to extend over the following 3-4 weeks as trapped-on-the-sidelines capital re-enters. The depth of the drawdown being escaped is itself a proxy for how much recovery fuel remains.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas treat drawdown depth as a level to fade (crossing its own MA, exceeding a weekday percentile) or as a recovery confirmed by range-deformation recoil. This one runs a breakout on the drawdown SERIES itself — entry fires when dd makes a fresh rolling-window high while still below zero — and adds a two-sided capacity band on the escaped trough (too shallow = no edge, too deep = overflow/regime break) drawn from the queue-capacity inspiration. Position size is driven by escaped drawdown depth, a distinct sizing signal from any prior idea.
idea.failure_mode: In a fast V-shaped recovery the drawdown series makes its fresh high only after price has already snapped most of the way back, so entries arrive late with little remaining recovery and the breakeven-then-trail exit gets whipsawed out near flat; conversely in a slow grinding bear the capacity band never disqualifies a dip fast enough and entries catch falling knives.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when upside semivariance dominates downside semivariance (laminar trend); rolling-high ATR trailing stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: A sustainable uptrend is 'laminar': its realized variance is concentrated on up-days while down-days stay small and orderly, whereas a fragile or topping trend is 'turbulent' with large down-shocks even as price drifts higher. Decomposing realized variance into upside and downside semivariance lets the upside share act as a direct measure of trend quality rather than trend magnitude. High upside-variance share predicts short-horizon continuation, and a single turbulent down-shock that breaks the laminar flow trips the rolling-high ATR trailing stop.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea decomposes realized volatility into upside vs downside semivariance and uses the upside-variance SHARE as the trend-strength signal. The list uses regression t-stats of price/OBV level, MA-slope streaks, range-compression coils, drawdown extremes, gap mechanics, and momentum percentiles - none partition variance by return sign. This measures trend quality through the asymmetry of the volatility distribution itself (laminar vs turbulent flow), a distinct mechanism from magnitude-, slope-, or compression-based trend detection.
idea.failure_mode: In a long steady bull market, upside-variance dominance is near-permanent, so the entry condition is satisfied almost continuously and the strategy degrades into buy-and-hold with extra commission/slippage drag; meanwhile the rolling-high ATR trail whipsaws on routine pullbacks, exiting and re-entering without improving risk-adjusted return.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Rank trading-day-of-month by trailing forward-return seasonality; enter long top-ranked calendar windows in 200-MA bull regime, breakeven-trail exit.
idea.hypothesis: Different trading-day-of-month positions (turn-of-month, mid-month settlement, options-expiry week) carry persistent forward-return tilts driven by structural cash flows, rebalancing, and payroll/benefit timing. By ranking each calendar position against the trailing-year distribution of all calendar positions' realized forward returns, the strategy enters only when today's calendar slot is currently among the seasonally strongest. Restricting this to a 200-day bull regime exploits that calendar tilts are reliably positive only when the broad trend already supports buyers.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior seasonality entries used day-of-month VOLATILITY norms, turn-of-month RANGE compression, per-weekday return t-statistics, or weekday-conditioned drawdown percentiles. This strategy instead builds a seasonal score per trading-day-of-month bucket from each bucket's trailing mean of already-realized H-day forward returns, then cross-ranks the current bucket against the trailing-year distribution of all buckets via a rolling percentile. The mechanism is a cross-calendar-position rank of forward-return seasonality, not a volatility, range, or single-weekday-statistic test.
idea.failure_mode: Trading-day-of-month forward-return tilts are weak and unstable; the rolling percentile rank may simply chase whichever calendar bucket had a recent lucky run, turning the supposed structural seasonality into noisy short-horizon bucket momentum that decays out of sample and gets eaten by commission and slippage on frequent 3-5 day trades.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Long/short when momentum across four horizons all rank-percentile high (or low) together for two confirming bars
idea.hypothesis: A momentum regime is trustworthy only when short-, medium-, and long-horizon return momentum all sit in the same tail of their own trailing distributions at once. Isolated single-horizon momentum is often noise or a transient spike, but agreement across the whole momentum term structure reflects a coherent, persistent drift that tends to continue for one to two weeks. Disagreement across horizons signals a momentum that is fragmenting and not worth trading.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas use a single rolling percentile (one momentum, one drawdown, one distance-from-MA) or AND two unrelated signals (price vs OBV t-stats). This mechanism builds a composite from FOUR momentum lookbacks, each independently percentile-ranked within its own trailing window, then averages those ranks into a term-structure agreement score. The tradeable quantity is cross-horizon rank breadth, not any single rank crossing — a distinct mechanism from every listed strategy.
idea.failure_mode: Multi-horizon momentum returns are highly collinear on a single index, so the four percentile ranks may move nearly in lockstep and the composite degenerates into a noisier copy of one long-horizon rank — adding computation but no real breadth information, while frequent long/short flips bleed edge to commissions and slippage.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Enter long when realized-vol z-score recoils back down through a tension threshold; inverse-vol size, fixed ATR vol-stop
idea.hypothesis: Equity realized volatility is strongly mean-reverting and negatively correlated with forward returns (the leverage/recovery effect). When realized vol has stretched well above its long-run equilibrium, it stores tension like a compressed spring; the bar where the vol z-score first recoils back below a threshold marks the spring releasing, and price recovery historically clusters in the days after that release. The strategy buys that volatility-decay onset rather than any price pattern.
idea.novelty_justification: Other list ideas that touch volatility wait for LOW/compressed vol then a breakout (true-range coil, turn-of-month range compression, leaky-bucket suppressed-vol deficit). This is the mechanical opposite: entry fires only after vol has been ELEVATED and is now crossing back DOWN through a tension threshold — trading the decay of high volatility, not the expansion of low volatility. The trigger is a pure realized-vol z-score crossover with no price/range/gap/drawdown/MA primitive involved, and sizing is inverse-vol (the volatility-targeting hallmark), which none of the compression ideas use.
idea.failure_mode: The vol z-score often crosses back below the threshold while price is still drifting down or chopping sideways — the negative vol/return relationship is noisy and lagged — so entries catch falling knives and the fixed (non-trailing) ATR stop gets tagged on the next leg down before the recovery materializes.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Fade overshoot gaps that follow long market-closure weekends/holidays, after two confirming fade bars
idea.hypothesis: Order flow accumulates while the market is closed, so the open-vs-prior-close gap printed on the first session after an extended closure (long weekend or holiday) tends to overshoot fair value. Once the closure-driven imbalance is absorbed, price drifts back toward the gap-fill. The longer the calendar closure, the larger the buildup and the stronger the subsequent reversion.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap/seasonality entries key off weekday climatology means, turn-of-month windows, or calendar percentiles. None uses closure length itself - the count of calendar days between consecutive trading sessions, derived purely from datetime-index spacing - as the seasonal primitive. The mechanism is 'fade the post-long-weekend/holiday gap', gated by closure duration, which is distinct from any weekday-mean or day-of-month profile in the list.
idea.failure_mode: Post-closure gaps following genuine new information (weekend macro news, holiday-period shocks) continue rather than revert; fading them then catches a trend and the fixed profit-target rarely fires, so most trades exit on the time-stop near the loss.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Switch between return-momentum and return-reversal based on the percentile rank of lag-1 return autocorrelation; fixed-bar exit
idea.hypothesis: Daily close-to-close returns alternate between persistent (shock-propagating) and anti-persistent (shock-dissipating) regimes, and the lag-1 serial correlation of returns measures which regime is active. When that autocorrelation sits in a high percentile of its own recent history, the last few days' return tends to continue; when it sits in a low percentile, the recent return tends to reverse. Trading direction conditional on the regime should capture both effects rather than betting on one permanently.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea computes the lag-1 autocorrelation coefficient of close-to-close RETURNS and uses its rolling-percentile rank to FLIP the trade between momentum and reversal. The closest entry uses autocorrelation of the high-low RANGE purely as a one-sided gate for momentum; this strategy makes the return-autocorrelation itself the regime classifier and emits opposite-direction trades in the two regimes — a switching mechanism, not a gate.
idea.failure_mode: Rolling lag-1 autocorrelation of daily SPY returns is extremely noisy and hovers near zero; its percentile rank may oscillate fast enough that the regime label is effectively random, producing whipsaw entries whose edge is eaten by commission and slippage.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Enter long when recent close-to-close returns stop touching their own historical percentile tails (return-distribution compression).
idea.hypothesis: When daily close-to-close returns repeatedly fail to reach their own long-run percentile tails, the return distribution has compressed into a low-energy 'coil' as participants stop pushing price to extremes. Equity indices have an upward drift, so coiled, tail-starved regimes tend to resolve upward once volatility re-expands. Trading the absence of tail events, rather than the size of moves, isolates compression directly from the return series.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas detect compression via high-low range, ATR, realized-vol std/z-score, or sub-median-range streaks. This strategy never measures range or volatility magnitude at all — it counts how many of the last W close-to-close returns crossed the empirical [low_pct, high_pct] quantile band of a long return history, and fires only when that tail-touch count collapses to zero. Compression is defined as the distribution failing to reach its own tails, a percentile-membership mechanism distinct from any variance/range estimator in the list, and gated by an explicit armed/disarmed hysteresis latch.
idea.failure_mode: Return-distribution compression is direction-agnostic: a coil can resolve downward just as easily as upward. The 200-MA bull filter and long-only stance lean on equity drift, but in choppy sideways markets the coil simply resolves into more chop, and the fixed 7-bar exit can dump the position straight into a downswing with no signal-based escape.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long while the lag-1 autocorrelation of intrabar close-location is positive and recent closes sit high in the range
idea.hypothesis: Where a bar's close lands inside its high-low range reveals which side won the day; when that close-location is autocorrelated across bars, one side holds durable, coherent control rather than control alternating in noise. SPY's structural drift means coherent upper-range control (positive close-location persistence plus an upward close-location bias) is a regime that tends to extend for several days before the coherence dissipates.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing range-autocorrelation ideas (#17) use lag-1 autocorrelation of the raw high-low range only as a gate for a separate return-direction trade, and #22 trades the intrabar close-location oscillator at its level with hysteresis. This strategy makes the lag-1 autocorrelation of the centered close-location series itself the binding signal — a second-order coherence property of where closes sit in the range — and derives direction from the sign of recent mean close-location. It is not a level trade and not a return-direction trade gated by range AC; the autocorrelation of an intrabar-position primitive is the mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: Lag-1 autocorrelation of close-location estimated on ~20 bars is a noisy statistic that hovers near zero, so the rho threshold crossing can be effectively random; with no bull-regime filter, brief coherent up-control can appear inside bear-market rallies and produce losing 3-5 day holds.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy the two-bar confirmed escape of distance-from-MA z-score out of a discount jam; exit at the mirror overbought z-band.
idea.hypothesis: When SPY trades persistently below its moving average it sits in a 'congestion jam' of accumulated discount; the back edge of that jam - the bar where the distance-from-MA z-score crosses back up out of the discount band - marks a regime where trapped sellers are exhausted and buyers begin to flow freely. Entering on the confirmed escape and riding until price reaches the symmetric overbought z-band captures the slow re-rating from discount to premium.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing z-score ideas in the list trade fresh momentum breakouts gated by an extension-count queue, or pair a z-score reclaim with true-range compression. This strategy uses neither: the entry primitive is the back-edge crossing of the z-score out of a discount jam (a shockwave dissipation boundary), and crucially the exit is a pure signal-reversal into the mirror overbought z-band rather than a fixed-bar, ATR-trail, or breakeven stop. No other idea pairs a discount-jam-escape entry with a symmetric premium-band reversal exit.
idea.failure_mode: In a sustained bear market the z-score can escape the lower band, drift sideways, and re-enter the jam repeatedly without ever reaching the upper exit band - leaving the position long through a slow grind down because the signal-reversal exit never fires until price becomes overbought.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Forced SI-epidemic model on distance-from-MA breakouts; long on infected-fraction ignition crossing, profit-target/time-stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: Breakouts propagate like contagions: a cluster of bars pushing price above its moving average makes the move visible and recruits further buying, but the cascade is self-limiting because the pool of un-positioned buyers depletes. Modeling recent >1-sigma breakout bars as infectious forcing on a susceptible-infected fraction lets the strategy detect the ignition of such a cascade. Entering at ignition, while the susceptible pool is still large, should capture the steep growth segment of the epidemic curve before saturation.
idea.novelty_justification: The closest prior idea gates z-score breakouts with a recent-extension count queue under a capacity ceiling. This strategy instead integrates a genuine forced SI dynamical system: a recursive contagion state where each breakout bar injects new infections scaled by the remaining susceptible fraction (1-I), against continuous recovery decay (-gamma*I). The tradable event is not the z-score itself nor a sliding-window counter, but the upward crossing of the smoothed infected-fraction curve through an ignition threshold during the epidemic's growth phase. That is a nonlinear state-variable mechanism, distinct from every count/queue/percentile/regression construct in the list.
idea.failure_mode: The SI accumulator smooths the raw breakout signal heavily, so ignition crossings lag the actual price breakout by roughly three bars. By the time the epidemic ignites, the bulk of the move may already be spent, causing entries into late-stage momentum that mean-reverts within the 1-2 week hold and makes the time-stop the dominant, losing exit.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Drawdown-gated leaky bucket of overnight down-gaps; long on capacity overflow, size scaled by overflow magnitude
idea.hypothesis: While SPY is in a drawdown, persistent overnight down-gaps reflect accumulating off-hours fear that drains the same buyers each session. When that accumulated overnight selling pressure exceeds the market's normal volatility-scaled tolerance, it signals near-term capitulation rather than a fresh trend. Bounces over a 3-4 week horizon then mean-revert the overshoot.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the existing leaky-bucket idea (suppressed-volatility deficit feeding a trend-following trigger with inverse-vol size), this bucket is fed exclusively by overnight down-gap magnitude and is gated to only accumulate while price sits in a drawdown. The trigger is a mean-reversion long when the bucket overflows a volatility-scaled capacity ceiling, and position size scales with how far past capacity the overflow ran. No other idea conditions overnight-gap accumulation on drawdown state or uses a capacity-overflow crossing as the capitulation signal.
idea.failure_mode: In a sustained bear leg (e.g. 2022), down-gaps accumulate and overflow capacity repeatedly, dragging the strategy long into ongoing declines; the 18-bar time-stop then forces exits at a loss before any recovery, producing death by a thousand cuts.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Enter long on a fresh up-gap only when the recent gap-sign balance is coherently upward; profit-target/time-stop exit
idea.hypothesis: Overnight gaps carry information, but a single gap is noisy and often fills intraday. When the recent stream of overnight gaps is directionally coherent (up-gaps consistently outnumber down-gaps), the gap process itself is in a persistent regime and a fresh up-gap is more likely to be continued rather than reverted over the next 1-2 sessions. Gap continuation should therefore be conditioned on the trend-strength of the gap sign sequence, not the gap magnitude.
idea.novelty_justification: Other gap ideas use a cumulative integral of gap magnitude (Mach number, second-derivative zero-cross), weekday climatology of gap means, or a leaky bucket of down-gaps. This strategy ignores gap magnitude entirely after the trigger: trend strength is measured as the count-balance of gap SIGNS over a window (a directional-coherence ratio in [-1,1]). The twist couples that coherence with a separate primitive - a fresh live up-gap today - and adds a consecutive-up-gap streak capacity cap to avoid chasing exhausted gap runs. No integral, no weekday term, no magnitude normalization.
idea.failure_mode: Index gaps in SPY have a strong mean-reverting (gap-fill) tendency; a coherent up-gap stream may simply mark an already-extended uptrend, so the fresh up-gap is bought near a local top and reverts. With a 1-2 day hold and a 1.5% target rarely reached at SPY volatility, most exits are time-stops capturing only small drift, and the edge can vanish after 2bps commission + 5bps slippage.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when range-path efficiency (net move over cumulative high-low range) crosses high, signal-scaled size, profit-target/time-stop exit
idea.hypothesis: A genuine trend covers ground efficiently: net displacement over a window is large relative to the total intrabar high-low range the price traversed to get there. When this range-path efficiency ratio is high and positive, the market is in a directional regime where price progress dominates intrabar churn, and that persistence tends to last 1-2 weeks before range expansion reabsorbs the move.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas use Kaufman-style close-diff efficiency, range compression coils, autocorrelation regimes, or envelope breakouts. None construct a trend-strength gauge by dividing net price displacement by the cumulative sum of high-low bar ranges as the 'path length' denominator. Using intrabar range (not close-to-close path) as the friction term is the distinct mechanism, and it doubles as the signal-scaled sizing weight.
idea.failure_mode: The high-low range denominator is itself a realized-volatility proxy, so the efficiency ratio is mechanically depressed in volatile genuine trends and inflated in quiet drifts; threshold crossings may cluster in low-vol regimes that subsequently mean-revert rather than trend, making the profit target rarely hit before the time-stop.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Drawdown hysteresis Schmitt-trigger: arm on deep drawdown, fire long on recovery cross, size scaled by trough depth; profit-target/time-stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: After SPY falls into a meaningful drawdown from a recent rolling peak, the first decisive recovery back toward the peak tends to attract trend-following and dip-buying flow, producing a multi-day continuation snapback. Shallow noise dips do not carry this flow, so a single threshold is too noisy; requiring the price to first reach a deep arm level and only then cross back up through a shallower fire level isolates genuine recoveries. The depth of the trough reached while armed proxies the magnitude of the mean-reversion pressure that has accumulated.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing drawdown ideas enter AT the deepening (fresh N-bar drawdown low, escaped-depth sizing), gate by weekday percentiles, or model range-deformation recoil and overnight-gap buckets. This strategy is a two-threshold Schmitt-trigger state machine on drawdown depth itself: it arms on a deep cross and fires the long only on the recovery cross back up through a separate shallower threshold. Entry timing is the recovery, not the deepening, and the hysteresis band (arm vs fire level) is the core mechanism rather than a percentile or a leaky bucket.
idea.failure_mode: In a sustained bear market the rolling-peak drawdown keeps deepening and the recovery cross fires repeatedly into dead-cat bounces; the 200-day trend filter mitigates this but lags, and during fast V-shaped recoveries the price can blow past the fire level before a bar prints inside the band, so the deepest, most profitable recoveries may never trigger an entry.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Inside-bar nesting density gauges a coil; trade the close breakout from the cluster, ATR-trailing exit
idea.hypothesis: When daily bars repeatedly nest inside their predecessor's range (true inside bars), the market is digesting information and storing directional potential rather than expressing it. A cluster of such nested bars represents a coiled spring; the first close that escapes above the cluster's recent range tends to continue for several days because suppressed two-sided activity has been resolved in one direction. Restricting this to bull regimes (price above the 200-day MA) and skipping the chaotic aftermath of a volatility spike keeps the entries clean.
idea.novelty_justification: Compression here is measured by the COUNT of inside bars (each bar geometrically contained within the prior bar's high-low range) in a trailing window - a relational nesting metric, not an absolute range-size metric. The listed 'consecutive sub-median-range bars' streak compares each bar's range to a median; the 'leaky-bucket suppressed-volatility deficit' accumulates an ATR shortfall; 'realized-vol z-score recoil' tracks a vol z-score. None use the inside-bar containment relationship between consecutive bars as the coil gauge, and none combine it with a same-bar close breakout from the nesting cluster.
idea.failure_mode: True inside bars are fairly uncommon on daily SPY, so requiring several of them in a short window AND a simultaneous fresh close breakout AND a bull regime AND no recent spike may produce very few trades per walk-forward fold, leaving the Sharpe estimate noisy and the strategy effectively untested out-of-sample.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Up-close streak crossing a yield-point count enters long, size scaled by accumulated ATR-strain, breakeven-then-trail exit
idea.hypothesis: A short run of consecutive up-closes behaves like elastic deformation - it tends to recoil (mean-revert). But once the streak crosses a 'yield-point' count, the trend has plastically deformed into a persistent regime where the displacement is unlikely to fully reverse. The amount of price ground covered while reaching that yield point (accumulated strain) measures how much directional energy the move carries, and thus how much conviction the position deserves.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing streak ideas count sub-median-range compression bars; existing elastic-deformation ideas track range recoil or vol z-score recoil. This strategy uses the consecutive up-close streak itself as a material under stress: the streak length is the deformation, a fixed count is the yield point separating elastic (no trade) from plastic (trade), and the price gain accumulated over exactly the yield-length window - normalized by ATR - is the 'plastic strain' that scales the position. No prior idea treats a streak count as a yield threshold or sizes by streak-window strain.
idea.failure_mode: Entering exactly when the up-close streak hits the yield count is structurally late - a 3-day winning streak is often near a short-term local top, so the breakeven-then-trail exit gets whipsawed by the normal pullback that follows multi-day runs. The strategy may systematically buy streak exhaustion rather than streak continuation.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Fade distance-from-MA z-score extremes only when its half-cycle autocorrelation is negative (predator-prey limit-cycle regime), 200-MA gated.
idea.hypothesis: When a market behaves like a predator-prey limit cycle, the distance-from-MA z-score oscillates rather than drifts, leaving a negative autocorrelation at a half-cycle lag (the series tends to sit on the opposite side of fair value ~L bars apart). In such a detectably cyclic regime, a deep z-score extreme is a cycle trough/peak that mean-reverts toward the opposite phase over the next 1-2 weeks. Outside that regime the same extreme carries no edge and is ignored.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior ideas either trade distance-from-MA z-score breakouts directly, or switch momentum/reversal off the lag-1 autocorrelation of returns. This one neither rides the z-score nor uses lag-1 return ACF: it computes the autocorrelation of the z-score itself at a deliberate half-cycle lag and uses a significantly NEGATIVE value as a positive detector of an oscillatory limit-cycle regime - a gate, not a switch - then fades extremes only inside it. The traded series, the lag semantics (half-cycle vs lag-1), and the predator-prey cycle-detection framing are all distinct.
idea.failure_mode: Rolling lagged autocorrelation is slow and noisy: by the time negative half-cycle ACF is statistically visible the oscillatory regime may already be decaying into a trend, so entries fire just as the cycle stops being a cycle - and a fade against a fresh trend bleeds until the time-stop.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Long when days-above-MA persistence streak crosses its own rolling percentile high; hysteresis re-arm, fixed ATR vol-stop.
idea.hypothesis: A trend that has held price above its moving average for an unusually long unbroken run (a high days-in-trend persistence streak) reflects durable institutional accumulation that tends to extend rather than immediately reverse. Measuring persistence by streak duration relative to its own recent distribution adapts the bar to each regime, since a 20-day streak is exceptional in choppy markets but ordinary in strong ones. The edge is buying confirmed, regime-relative trend strength while excluding shallow trends that merely poke above the average.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior streak strategies count consecutive up-closes (gains) or consecutive sub-median-range bars (compression). This counts consecutive bars price stays above its MA - a duration-of-trend measure, not a gain or volatility measure - and the entry bar is the streak length's own rolling percentile rank, not a fixed count or a yield point. Hysteresis comes from an arm/disarm percentile band that blocks re-entry until the streak rank collapses below a lower threshold, distinct from any listed Schmitt-trigger or lockout mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: By the time a days-above-MA streak reaches a high percentile, the trend is already mature; entering late-stage trends exposes the strategy to exhaustion reversals, and the fixed (non-trailing) ATR stop gives back open profit before the trend-break exit fires.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Long healthy pullbacks when the volatility infection burns out: SI-style decay of excess-ATR below half its drawdown peak.
idea.hypothesis: A pullback inside a bull market behaves like an epidemic: a price shock 'infects' the tape with elevated volatility, the infection peaks, then burns out as forced sellers are exhausted. The reliable recovery edge is not the price low but the moment the volatility infection has visibly decayed (excess ATR falls back through half its in-drawdown peak), because that signals the susceptible-seller pool is spent while trend buyers remain.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas trade vol z-score recoiling through a tension threshold, drawdown Schmitt-triggers on price recovery crosses, or SI-epidemic ignition on distance-from-MA breakouts. This strategy instead models the *removed/recovery* phase of an SI curve: it normalizes excess ATR (ATR% above its rolling-median baseline) against its own rolling peak to form an explicit infected-fraction, and fires only on the fresh downward cross of that fraction — a volatility-burnout trigger, not a price-recovery or vol-z-score trigger, and gated to drawdowns occurring above the 200-day MA.
idea.failure_mode: In a genuine bear market the volatility infection also decays as price grinds lower; the 200-MA filter screens most of this, but a slow drawdown that keeps close marginally above a falling MA can still fire entries into continued weakness, and the ATR trailing stop will whipsaw out repeatedly.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy convalescence: enter when drawdown-level percentile AND infected-fraction percentile both agree after a prior epidemic arm
idea.hypothesis: After a real selloff, SPY tends to drift higher during the convalescent phase when the drawdown has visibly stopped deepening and the share of recently-stressed bars is collapsing. Treating bars below a drawdown threshold as 'infected', an SI-style infected fraction that ranks low while the raw drawdown ranks high marks the moment recovery is statistically confirmed but not yet exhausted, capturing the post-panic relief drift before mean conditions reassert.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior drawdown ideas use single-primitive level thresholds: the Schmitt-trigger arms on a deep drawdown LEVEL and fires on a price recovery cross; the leaky-bucket accumulates down-gaps; the SI-burnout idea decays excess-ATR below half its peak. This strategy's core is a two-primitive AND of two distinct ROLLING PERCENTILE RANKS — the drawdown level's own rank and the rank of an SI infected-fraction (rolling mean of a drawdown-threshold indicator). Neither primitive is a level threshold; both are distributional ranks, and they measure different things (how shallow the drawdown is vs. how much the epidemic has subsided). The epidemic-arm gate is a state requirement, not the signal.
idea.failure_mode: The AND of 'drawdown rank high' and 'infected-fraction rank low' may rarely co-occur cleanly — by the time the infected fraction ranks low the recovery is often already complete, so entries fire late and few, leaving too few trades for a meaningful Sharpe. Secondarily, the signal-reversal exit holds purely on the rank condition, so a renewed downturn can keep the strategy long well past the 3-5 day target if ranks decay slowly.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Recent down-gap disturbance primes a long that fires when price ROC-acceleration releases upward; 200-MA gated, signal-reversal exit
idea.hypothesis: An overnight down-gap acts like a braking car in traffic: it injects a local disturbance that compresses subsequent price flow. In a bull regime that compression typically resolves upward, and the resolution announces itself as a release shockwave - the rate-of-change of price stops decelerating and begins to accelerate. Buying the rising edge of ROC-acceleration after a fresh down-gap disturbance captures the front of that release wave for a 1-2 day move.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas use a cumulative-gap integral and its second-derivative zero-cross, leaky-bucket accumulators of down-gaps, calendar-conditioned gap fades, or up-gap sign-balance continuation. This strategy uses no accumulator and no gap integral: a discrete down-gap event simply opens a short-lived priming window (a rolling-window event gate), and the actual directional trigger is the acceleration of the close-price rate-of-change - not of the gap series. The mechanism is event-primed-gate plus price-ROC-acceleration rising edge, distinct from every continuous gap-curvature or bucket mechanism listed.
idea.failure_mode: SPY down-gaps in a bull regime often mean-revert within a single bar, so by the time ROC-acceleration confirms a positive rising edge the easy 1-2 day bounce is already spent, leaving the entry buying exhaustion. Short roc/accel periods also make the acceleration sign whippy, generating many one-bar trades whose small edge is consumed by commission and slippage.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Greenshields kinematic-wave speed flips sign as a drawdown jam clears the half-density point; trade the reversion.
idea.hypothesis: A deep drawdown behaves like a traffic jam: price 'density' (drawdown depth) builds backward and self-reinforces while the road is congested, but once depth falls back through the flow-maximizing critical density the disturbance dissipates as a forward free-flow wave. The LWR/Greenshields kinematic-wave speed dq/drho = 1 - 2*rho flips from negative (jam growing) to positive (jam clearing) exactly at rho = rho_max/2, and that sign flip marks the moment an over-compressed move begins reverting toward its prior extreme.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing drawdown ideas use raw extremum prints, Schmitt-trigger hysteresis with two hand-set arm/disarm thresholds, leaky-bucket accumulators, or epidemic infected-fraction models. This uses neither thresholds nor accumulators: it maps drawdown depth onto the Greenshields fundamental diagram, where the only critical point is the flow-maximizing density rho_max/2, and trades the SIGN CHANGE of the kinematic wave speed 1-2*rho. The trigger is a regime-boundary crossing derived from a traffic-flow model, not a tuned level, and it is symmetric: a run-up 'anti-jam' above the rolling trough provides the short side.
idea.failure_mode: rho_max is a trailing rolling maximum of depth, so the critical 0.5 line drifts purely from window bookkeeping as old deep bars age out of the lookback. The wave-speed flip can therefore fire from normalization drift rather than genuine price recovery, producing entries when SPY has barely moved and the 'reversion' never materializes.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy drawdowns whose increment series turns anti-persistent (jam-dissipating autocorrelation) in a 200-MA bull regime
idea.hypothesis: A price drawdown is a congestion wave: when the bar-to-bar changes in drawdown depth become anti-persistent (negative lag-1 autocorrelation), each push deeper is reliably met by a recovery push, signalling the shockwave is dissipating rather than propagating. Markets in a structural uptrend resolve such oscillating drawdowns upward, so buying while the jam is provably clearing captures the recovery leg before price reclaims its prior high.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea computes the lag-1 autocorrelation of the drawdown-increment series itself. The closest entries use return-autocorrelation regime switching, fresh-drawdown extremes, drawdown Schmitt-triggers, or a Greenshields density-speed model. Here the discriminating quantity is the persistence sign of dd.diff() inside an open drawdown - measuring whether congestion is propagating or dissipating - which is a distinct mechanism from depth, density, or return-based autocorrelation.
idea.failure_mode: Anti-persistent drawdown increments may simply describe choppy sideways grinding rather than a genuine recovery; entries could fire repeatedly into a slow bleed where each small bounce is followed by a slightly lower low, and the 3*ATR trailing stop then leaks commission and slippage on a string of small losers.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Volume-confirmed up-gap treated as an infection event in an un-extended (susceptible) market, signal-scaled size, profit-target/time-stop exit
idea.hypothesis: An overnight up-gap accompanied by a genuine volume surge reflects informed accumulation rather than noise, so it tends to continue over the following 1-2 weeks instead of filling. The effect is strongest when the market is still 'susceptible' - not already stretched far above its medium-term mean - because an extended market has little remaining fuel for the move to propagate. Volume confirmation separates real demand shocks from thin overnight drift.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas key off cumulative-gap second derivatives, weekday gap climatology, post-holiday overshoot fades, down-gap leaky buckets, gap-sign coherence, or ROC-acceleration release. None use a volume surge as the confirming primitive, and none gate entry on a susceptibility (un-extension) condition. The mechanism here is a two-factor infection trigger - statistically large gap AND volume surge - admitted only into a susceptible market, with continuous infection intensity (gap z times volume excess) driving position size. That is a distinct trigger and a distinct sizing channel from every prior idea.
idea.failure_mode: Volume surges on up-gaps in SPY are frequently exhaustion or news-driven spikes that mark short-term tops; the gap then fills within days, so the 1-2 week continuation premise inverts and the fixed profit target is rarely reached before the time-stop forces a flat-to-losing exit.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Enter long when realized vol sits below its calendar-month climatological norm AND that month is seasonally bullish
idea.hypothesis: Volatility has a calendar-month seasonality of its own: each month has a typical 'season-normal' realized-vol level, and when current vol contracts below that month's historical norm the market is in an 'elastic' (reversible, drift-friendly) regime rather than a 'plastic' (regime-shifting) one. Combining this elastic-vol condition with the independent fact that the same calendar month also carries a positive historical mean return isolates quiet, seasonally tailwinded windows where upward drift is most reliable. Requiring both to agree filters out quiet-but-unfavorable and favorable-but-turbulent periods.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing seasonality entries rank trading-day-of-month by forward return or use weekday climatological gap means; none compare CURRENT realized volatility to its own per-calendar-month climatological average. The primary primitive here is a volatility-vs-seasonal-vol-norm ratio (the 'elastic regime' detector), gated by an independently computed month-of-year return-sign primitive. No prior idea builds a month-of-year volatility climatology or uses elastic-vs-plastic deformation as a vol-contraction regime test.
idea.failure_mode: With only ~10 years of data each calendar month has roughly 10 same-month samples, so the climatological volatility and return estimates are noisy and unstable across WFO windows; the two-primitive AND may simply shrink the trade count without delivering a real seasonal edge, since both monthly vol and monthly returns are only weakly seasonal in SPY.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Volume-confirmed thrust regime: net up/down confirmed-bar count switches to accumulation, fire long on confirmed up-bar, refractory after volume climax
idea.hypothesis: Sustained advances are accompanied by an imbalance of up-moves occurring on above-average volume versus down-moves on volume; this imbalance defines an 'accumulation regime' where institutional demand is genuine. Buying a fresh volume-confirmed up-bar inside that regime aligns entries with effort-backed demand rather than thin drift. A single outsized volume climax, however, often marks short-term exhaustion, so a refractory pause after a spike avoids buying into a blow-off.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior list entries that touch volume treat a single volume-confirmed up-GAP as an SI infection event. This strategy ignores gaps entirely and instead builds a two-state regime classifier from the rolling NET count of volume-confirmed up-bars minus volume-confirmed down-bars, an effort-vs-result tally. The entry primitive (intraday volume-confirmed up close) is distinct from gap mechanics, and the refractory-after-climax filter is a volume-magnitude exhaustion gate not present in any listed idea.
idea.failure_mode: The net volume-confirmed count is largely coincident with price, so by the time the accumulation regime is confirmed the easy move is done; the confirmed up-bar trigger then buys local highs and the ATR trailing stop whipsaws out repeatedly in choppy-but-rising tape, bleeding via commissions and slippage.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Yield-point momentum: enter when ATR-strain crosses the elastic limit and the deformation stays plastic (retained); breakeven-trail exit
idea.hypothesis: Small price moves are elastic - they spring back like a loaded spring releasing energy, producing noise rather than trend. Once a move's total range in ATR units exceeds a yield strain, the deformation becomes plastic: the advance is retained through minor pullbacks instead of snapping back to origin. Plastically deformed markets carry genuine momentum that persists for one to two weeks before the next yield event.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas use up-close streak COUNTS as a yield criterion, vol-infection burnout, ROC-acceleration release, or distance-from-MA z-scores. This strategy's discriminator is a materials-science unloading test: it decomposes the recent excursion into retained plastic strain (close above the window low) versus elastic recovery (springback from the window high), and only fires when total excursion crosses a yield threshold AND the plastic fraction dominates AND absolute springback stays capped. The mechanism is a strain-partition gate, not a count, a streak, or a z-score crossing.
idea.failure_mode: The yield strain threshold is regime-sensitive: in a persistently low-volatility uptrend, total ATR-unit excursion rarely crosses the yield point, so the strategy almost never trades; in a volatile chop it crosses the yield point on moves that still mean-revert, since high plastic_ratio merely means the close sits near the window high - a condition that whipsaws when ATR expands faster than price.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when return-dispersion compresses to a low percentile AND a return breaks out beyond that compressed band; fixed-bar exit.
idea.hypothesis: When close-to-close return dispersion contracts into a low percentile of its own history, the market is 'jammed' - participants are bunched in a low-velocity coil. A return that punches above this compressed band is a shockwave front: the jam releasing in a directional wave. The compressed-energy release tends to carry price for a bar or two before dissipating.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the 'stop touching percentile tails' idea (which watches the return distribution's tails directly) or the 'inside-bar nesting density' coil (which uses high-low ranges and an ATR trailing exit), this strategy gauges compression via the rolling STD of close-to-close returns ranked against its own history, and requires a second, separate primitive - a return exceeding k times that compressed dispersion (escape velocity) with positive recent cumulative return. Both the coil state and the breakout must AND. Exit is purely fixed-bar, no path-dependent trail or signal exit, distinguishing it from every trailing/profit-target variant in the list.
idea.failure_mode: Compression is direction-agnostic: a released coil can break down just as easily as up. The cum_release>0 filter biases entries toward very-short-term momentum that frequently mean-reverts inside the 1-2 day hold window, so the breakout primitive may simply buy local tops that fade before the fixed-bar exit fires.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade drawdown recoveries when overnight gaps stop being filled intraday and start being retained to the close (elastic-to-plastic transition).
idea.hypothesis: During a drawdown, most relief gaps are 'elastic' - they get filled back intraday as dip-buyers fade and trapped longs sell into strength, so the gap leaves no permanent footprint. Genuine recovery begins when up-gaps become 'plastic': they are retained through the close because the marginal buyer now defends the open. A rising fraction of retained up-gaps therefore marks the structural turn that a single big gap or simple gap count cannot distinguish from a dead-cat bounce.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas accumulate gap magnitude (leaky bucket), measure gap-sign balance, fade post-holiday overshoot gaps, or treat a volume gap as an infection event. None measure gap RETENTION - the intraday fate of the gap (open-to-close holding vs reversion). The mechanism here is the elastic/plastic ratio: what fraction of overnight gaps survive to the close, used as a regime transition detector inside a drawdown. That is a distinct primitive from gap size, gap count, or gap sign.
idea.failure_mode: Conditional retention ratios are noisy on daily SPY because gap-bar counts inside a 30-bar window are small; the up_ret series can cross the threshold on just one or two extra retained gaps, producing whipsaw entries that the 3-4 week hold then locks into stale recovery signals.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when close sits above its rolling median by k ATRs; mirror exit when k ATRs below median, refractory after vol spikes.
idea.hypothesis: Equity index trends persist for several weeks once price establishes a clear positional lead over its recent typical level. Measuring that lead against the rolling median (a robust positional statistic) in ATR units filters out noise-driven excursions, and a symmetric mirror threshold ensures we only abandon the trend on a genuine, volatility-significant reversal rather than an ordinary pullback.
idea.novelty_justification: Relative position is measured against the rolling MEDIAN (a rank/positional statistic), normalized by ATR, with a symmetric +-k Schmitt band whose exit is the literal mirror of the entry. No list item uses a median-centered, ATR-normalized position oscillator. The closest entry (z-score escape from a discount jam) uses mean/std and is a reversion-from-oversold construction with opposite polarity; this one is a trend-continuation entry from above the median.
idea.failure_mode: The rolling median updates slowly, so on a sharp V-shaped reversal the mirror exit at -k ATRs fires far too late, surrendering most of a multi-week gain in a single large round-trip.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade distance-from-MA z-score de-seasonalized against its trailing month-of-year climatology; two-bar confirmed discount entry, mirror-flip exit.
idea.hypothesis: SPY's distance-from-MA z-score carries a calendar-month component: in some months price systematically trades richer or cheaper relative to its moving average for reasons unrelated to genuine mispricing (tax flows, fund rebalancing windows, summer drift). A raw z-score therefore conflates seasonal richness with a true discount. Subtracting each month's trailing climatological z leaves a residual anomaly that more cleanly isolates an out-of-character discount, which historically mean-reverts upward over multi-week horizons.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike 'two-bar confirmed escape of distance-from-MA z-score out of a discount jam' which trades the raw z-score, this strategy de-seasonalizes z by subtracting an expanding trailing month-of-year climatology of the z-score itself before thresholding. The other seasonality entries in the list rank trading-day-of-month by forward returns or compare realized vol to a calendar norm; here the calendar climatology is applied as a de-trending operator on the signal primitive, so entries fire on a discount relative to seasonal expectation rather than an absolute one. No prior idea removes a seasonal mean from the z-score before mean-reverting it.
idea.failure_mode: SPY's distance-from-MA z-score may simply not have a stable month-of-year bias; if so, the climatology subtraction injects estimation noise (especially in early years when each month bucket has few prior observations) instead of removing real seasonal structure, leaving the strategy a noisier version of plain z-score mean reversion with no edge after costs.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when two consecutive down-gaps are intraday-rejected (bought back) during a drawdown; profit-target/time-stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: During a drawdown, overnight sellers repeatedly gap price down at the open, but when real demand has returned, those down-gaps get bought back intraday and the bar closes well above its open. Two such absorbed down-gaps in a row signal that buyers are actively defending the discount rather than capitulating, marking the inflection of a short-horizon recovery.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas are an overflowing leaky bucket of down-gaps (accumulator), up-gap continuation with coherent gap-sign balance, or gaps that stop being filled and get retained to the close (elastic-to-plastic). This strategy uses the opposite primitive: it requires down-gaps to be actively REJECTED — open below prior close but close recovering a high fraction of the gap — as a failed-breakdown / buyer-defense signal. The mechanism is intraday gap-recovery ratio, not accumulation, not retention, not up-gap momentum.
idea.failure_mode: In genuine drawdowns, intraday-bought down-gaps are frequently dead-cat bounces that immediately precede the next leg down, so two-bar confirmation can systematically enter just ahead of continuation selling, and a 2% profit target rarely fills within a 2-bar window when volatility is elevated — leaving most trades to exit flat-to-down on the time-stop.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy selling exhaustion when intrabar range contracts while closes keep pinning near the low
idea.hypothesis: During a pullback, sellers initially produce wide-range bars with closes near the low; as the move matures the daily high-low range contracts even though closes still finish weak. This divergence - persistently bearish close-location combined with a shrinking range - signals that downside pressure is fading rather than accelerating, and price tends to revert upward over the following 3-5 days.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea combines the LEVEL of intrabar close-location-value with intrabar high-low range contraction. Item #6 uses close-location autocorrelation for momentum, not its level for reversion; vol-burnout items (#16,#17) use realized close-to-close volatility/excess-ATR, not the contraction of raw daily range versus a longer range baseline. The core mechanism here is a divergence detector between two purely intrabar range-derived series, gated by a signal-to-noise filter on close-location stability.
idea.failure_mode: In a slow low-volatility grind-down the daily range contracts AND closes pin low simultaneously with no bounce - the exact trap. The strategy buys repeatedly into a quiet downtrend, and because ATR is computed from the already-contracted range, the fixed vol-stop sits tight and gets clipped, bleeding equity through commissions and slippage.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Shockwave front speed: net displacement measured in ATRs-per-bar drives a long/short stop-and-reverse
idea.hypothesis: A trend's strength is best read as a propagation speed: the net directional ground covered per bar, measured in units of current volatility (ATR). When this 'front speed' clears a band, price is moving like an uncongested shockwave front and tends to keep advancing in that direction for weeks; when it collapses through the opposite band the front has reversed. Trading the front direction captures persistent volatility-normalized trend travel.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the listed range-path efficiency idea (net move over cumulative high-low path, long-only, profit-target exit), this uses ATR itself as the measuring stick and reframes the metric as a kinematic propagation speed (ATRs of net travel per bar). No listed idea measures trend as a velocity in volatility units, and none uses a pure long/short stop-and-reverse where the only exit is the opposite entry firing. It is not a vol-recoil, autocorrelation-regime, drawdown-epidemic, gap, or streak mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: Always-in-market stop-and-reverse: in choppy, directionless regimes the front speed oscillates around the +/- threshold band, triggering repeated reversals that each pay commission and slippage with no sustained trend to recoup them.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Detect anomalous close-to-close return jumps, enter long only if price retains the new level for two bars (plastic deformation), ATR rolling-high trailing exit
idea.hypothesis: A close-to-close return that is large relative to recent return volatility is a discontinuity (a 'gap' in the close path) caused by an information shock. If the market is merely noisy, price snaps back elastically and the jump dissipates; if the shock genuinely repriced the asset, the post-jump level is held and deformation is plastic. Jumps whose new level survives two retention bars exhibit short-horizon continuation that naive momentum filters miss because they react to direction rather than to level retention.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas use actual open-vs-prior-close overnight gaps, gap-sign balance, gap fills, or epidemic/leaky-bucket framings. This defines the gap entirely within the close-to-close return series as a one-bar return discontinuity (return z-score), and its confirmation is not 'two up-close bars' or 'two fade bars' but a strict path-dependent retention test: both following closes must stay above jump_close minus k*ATR. The elastic/plastic distinction is operationalized as level retention rather than streak counting (yield-point/ATR-strain idea) or ATR-strain accumulation. Mechanism = jump-detection + level-retention confirmation, which appears nowhere in the list.
idea.failure_mode: Large positive close-to-close returns in equity indices are disproportionately bear-market relief rallies; the two-bar retention filter will frequently confirm right before a continuation of the underlying decline, and the tight ATR trailing stop will then exit at a loss. The strategy may systematically buy dead-cat bounces.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Donchian channel breakout that fires only when ATR is actively expanding above its baseline; 200-MA regime gate, fixed ATR vol-stop
idea.hypothesis: Channel breakouts that occur while volatility is contracting tend to be liquidity-driven noise that mean-reverts, whereas breakouts coinciding with an igniting volatility regime reflect a genuine shift in the participant pool and tend to extend. By requiring ATR to both exceed its own multi-week baseline and be rising versus several bars ago, the strategy only commits when the 'volatility predator' is actively surging on top of the price-range 'prey', filtering the breakout population down to the self-sustaining cohort.
idea.novelty_justification: The already-generated list is dominated by volatility/range COMPRESSION setups (inside-bar coils, return-dispersion compression, distribution compression) that trade the escape from a quiet state. This strategy inverts that logic: it is a classic high/low channel breakout that is explicitly REQUIRED to occur during volatility EXPANSION (ATR above baseline AND ATR accelerating). No listed idea uses channel breakout, none gates on ATR acceleration as the confirming primitive, and none pairs that with a fixed (non-trailing) ATR vol-stop anchored at entry.
idea.failure_mode: Requiring volatility to already be expanding means entries arrive late — after a meaningful portion of the move and at the worst point for slippage. In sharp V-shaped reversals the breakout+expansion confirmation fires near a local extreme, and the fixed entry-anchored vol-stop (which never tightens) then gives the position too much room to bleed back through the stop, producing a string of full-k*ATR losers.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Cumulative overnight-gap momentum reservoir, percentile-ranked; long while it stays in its high band, hysteresis exit.
idea.hypothesis: Equity drift is concentrated in the overnight session, and that overnight (gap) momentum is itself autocorrelated: a run of positive open-vs-prior-close gaps tends to be followed by more of them. By summing recent overnight returns into a 'reservoir' and ranking that reservoir against its own trailing distribution, we identify regimes where overnight buying pressure is in its own high band and ride them while they persist.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas count gap signs for coherence, accumulate down-gaps in a leaky bucket for mean-reversion, or wait for ROC-acceleration to release a primed long. This strategy instead explicitly decomposes return into its overnight component, builds a continuous cumulative overnight-momentum oscillator, and gates trend-following purely on that oscillator's self-referential percentile rank - a momentum-dominance mechanism, not sign-counting, overflow-accumulation, or acceleration-release.
idea.failure_mode: Percentile entry buys when overnight momentum is already in its top band, which often coincides with short-term exhaustion; the lagging lower-percentile exit band then holds the position through the reversal, giving systematically late exits and buying tops.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Long when an overnight up-gap breaches the high percentile of its own weekday's trailing gap climatology; fixed-bar exit
idea.hypothesis: Overnight gaps in SPY carry a day-of-week seasonal signature: weekdays differ systematically in how news and order flow accumulate over their preceding non-trading interval. A gap that is large relative to its own weekday's recent distribution signals an unusually strong information shock for that calendar slot, which tends to be followed through over the next one to two weeks rather than fully reverted. Comparing each gap only to same-weekday history removes the structural day-of-week bias and isolates the genuine anomaly.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing seasonality ideas condition on month-of-year climatology (realized-vol norms, de-seasonalized z-scores); none use day-of-week. Existing gap ideas use leaky buckets of down-gaps, gap-sign balance, up-gap infection events, or gap fill-vs-retain dynamics — all measured against the gap's own magnitude or sign history. This strategy is the only one that builds a separate trailing gap distribution per weekday and fires when the current gap exceeds the high percentile of that specific weekday's climatology. The mechanism is weekday-conditional tail detection, not magnitude or balance.
idea.failure_mode: Day-of-week gap effects in SPY are weak and unstable, and each weekday accumulates only ~gap_window samples, making the percentile threshold noisy and prone to chasing idiosyncratic single gaps. Overnight gaps are also among the most efficiently priced features of SPY, so any residual edge may not survive 2bps commission plus 5bps slippage.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Loaded-spring breakout: leaky-integrator tension from range-percentile compression, fired on percentile breakout cross.
idea.hypothesis: When SPY's close gets pinned in the lower band of its trailing range-percentile for several consecutive bars, a coiled 'restoring tension' builds while latent demand accumulates. A sudden percentile-rank breakout to the upper band releases that stored tension, producing a short-lived 1-2 day overshoot. Slow drifts to new highs lack stored tension and tend to stall, so requiring recent compression filters genuine spring releases from grinding momentum.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the dispersion-compression item (which compresses realized volatility) this compresses price's *position* within its trailing range expressed as a rolling percentile rank, and accumulates compression duration in a leaky IIR integrator (tension = decay*prev + compressed_flag) that both gates entry and scales size. No listed idea uses a leaky-integrator of range-percentile-band residency time as a stored-energy gauge, nor combines it with a percentile-rank crossing as the release trigger. It is not a streak count, not a drawdown, not intrabar pinning, not a z-score escape.
idea.failure_mode: The tightest failure is that a percentile rank jumping from the bottom quartile to the top 80% in one or two bars is itself a rare, violent event that often marks a short-term exhaustion top rather than continuation — the spring may have already fully released into the breakout bar, leaving no overshoot for the shifted next-bar fill to capture.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Two-state elastic spring on distance-from-MA z-score: load at percentile extreme, fire long/short on velocity recoil, breakeven-then-trail exit.
idea.hypothesis: When price stretches far from its moving average it stores 'elastic potential' relative to its own recent distribution; the snap-back is most reliably tradable not at the extreme itself but the moment the stretch begins to recoil (z-score velocity flips sign). During an active drawdown this recoil is a genuine recovery impulse, while near highs a downward recoil is an overextension fade. Entering on the release rather than the extreme avoids catching falling/rising knives.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas fade z-score extremes directly (item 8), de-seasonalize z (item 22), or use drawdown LEVEL in Schmitt triggers (item 5).
