This uses a distinct two-state spring machine: the displacement (z below/above its own rolling percentile) only ARMS the spring; the actual trade fires on the sign of z-velocity (recoil), and the spring resets if z crosses zero without recoiling. The loaded-spring item (30) uses range-percentile compression with a leaky integrator, a different primitive. No prior idea separates 'displacement arms' from 'velocity fires' on a distance-from-MA z-score.
idea.failure_mode: z-velocity turning positive is a noisy trigger — in a sustained downtrend the spring arms then fires on every one-bar bounce, repeatedly entering longs that get stopped out as price keeps grinding lower. The drawdown gate helps but does not distinguish a recoverable dip from the start of a regime collapse.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Higher-lows staircase streak builds a support coil; breakout fires long when close clears the staircase ceiling, profit-target/time-stop exit
idea.hypothesis: A run of consecutive higher lows carves a visible accumulation staircase where buyers defend progressively higher prices. When price finally closes above the ceiling of that staircase, the supply overhang there has been cleared and short-horizon continuation is likely. The structural base limits the breakout's failure room, giving a favorable 1-2 day risk profile.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior streak ideas count up-closes, up-down confirmed bars, days-above-MA persistence, or ATR-strain crossings. This counts consecutive HIGHER LOWS - a rising-support staircase - which is a structural price-geometry streak, not a return/close/MA streak. The breakout trigger is close clearing the staircase's own high ceiling, and the streak counter itself carries a hysteresis tolerance band so micro-dips don't reset it. No listed idea uses a higher-low staircase as the coil.
idea.failure_mode: Rising-low staircases that finally break their ceiling frequently mark local exhaustion rather than continuation - the last marginal buyers have already committed. The pattern is also textbook-obvious and likely front-run, so the 1-2 day edge may be whipsawed away, especially when the tolerance band keeps a deteriorating streak artificially alive.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when range-fill level enters upper band below capacity AND ROC acceleration is positive; mirror-flip exit
idea.hypothesis: Treating a rolling high-low range as a buffer with finite capacity, the position of price within that buffer (fill level) measures absorbed buying pressure. A genuine 1-2 week advance needs the buffer to be filling toward, but not yet overflowing, capacity AND the momentum feeding it to be accelerating, not just positive. Entries that satisfy only one primitive are either stale moves running on inertia or fast spikes with no underlying base.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas either break OUT of a Donchian/range channel or fade z-score extremes; this strategy trades the INTERIOR fill fraction of the range as a capacity buffer and never references a breakout. It pairs that relative-position primitive with second-derivative ROC acceleration under a strict two-primitive AND, and uses a true signal-reversal exit that fires only when the mirror (bearish-flipped) condition holds, producing genuine multi-day holds distinct from the median-ATR mirror-exit and the loaded-spring/leaky-integrator entries in the list.
idea.failure_mode: The two-primitive AND plus the overflow ceiling makes entries rare; in strong trending years SPY's fill level pins near the capacity ceiling and the overflow guard blocks participation, so the strategy may sit flat through the best advances and only trade choppy mid-range conditions where the mirror exit whipsaws.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when z-score rebound velocity beats the peak descent velocity that caused the drawdown (elastic recoil); profit-target/time-stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: When price sits below a short MA, the speed at which the distance-from-MA z-score is recovering relative to the fastest single-bar drop that created the dip distinguishes an elastic snapback from plastic (permanent) damage. A rebound velocity that meets or exceeds the prior descent velocity signals stored mean-reversion energy releasing, whereas a slow drift back signals genuine repricing. Markets that fall fast and start rising at a comparable rate tend to overshoot back toward fair value over the following days.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing drawdown-recovery ideas trigger on level crossings (Schmitt-trigger hysteresis), percentile bands, autocorrelation regimes, or a single velocity recoil. This strategy's mechanism is a strain-rate RATIO: it explicitly compares the current upward slope of the z-score against the rolling maximum downward slope within the same dip. The entry is gated on rebound-speed being a fraction of fall-speed - an asymmetry test no listed idea performs. Nothing else measures descent velocity and recovery velocity as a comparative quotient.
idea.failure_mode: With only a 5-bar MA and a 4-bar descent window, the z-score is extremely noisy; a fast rebound bar is often just bid-ask bounce or a one-day dead-cat bounce rather than true elastic energy, so the recoil ratio fires on noise and the 5-bar time-stop exits flat-to-negative after commissions and slippage.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: In low-percentile-range neap-tide compression, buy the lower channel node for mean reversion to center
idea.hypothesis: When the rolling percentile of true range collapses into a low band, the market behaves like a standing wave with a small amplitude envelope: price is node-bounded and intra-channel displacement reverts rather than breaks out. A close pinned in the bottom of its recent high-low channel during such a 'neap tide' regime tends to drift back toward the channel center over 1-2 days, because suppressed volatility removes the breakout fuel that would otherwise carry it through the node.
idea.novelty_justification: Every prior range-compression idea in the list trades the breakout OUT of the compressed band (loaded-spring breakout, return-dispersion compression breakout, inside-bar coil breakout, Donchian-with-ATR-expansion). This strategy does the opposite: it trades mean reversion WITHIN the compressed band, treating low range-percentile as the precondition that makes intra-channel reversion reliable rather than as a coil that must explode. The signal is the close's percentile position inside the channel, gated by range percentile being low - not a breakout cross, not a drawdown/epidemic mechanic, not a z-score-vs-MA play.
idea.failure_mode: Compression often precedes directional expansion, not reversion - if a low range-percentile resolves into a downside breakout, buying the lower channel node walks straight into the move and the initial ATR stop takes a full loss before the breakeven trigger can ever arm.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Short when overnight up-gap streak diverges from intraday red-body streak (and mirror long); breakeven-then-trail exit
idea.hypothesis: When the market keeps gapping up overnight but each session closes red, overnight enthusiasm is no longer being held by intraday participants - distribution dressed as strength. The opposite divergence (persistent down-gaps that are repeatedly bought back to a green close) signals quiet accumulation. The two consecutive-streak counts behave like a loaded spring whose two ends pull in opposite directions; when both streaks build far enough the elastic snaps and price recoils against the gap direction over the next day or two.
idea.novelty_justification: The already-generated gap ideas use cumulative gap magnitude reservoirs, gap-fill-vs-retention transitions, weekday gap climatology percentiles, or single down-gap-rejection events. This strategy instead tracks TWO independent consecutive-streak counts - the run-length of same-direction overnight gaps AND the run-length of same-direction intraday candle bodies - and trades only their divergence (gaps one way, bodies the other). No prior idea counts an overnight-gap-direction streak against an intraday-body-direction streak as separately-computed primitives, nor uses their disagreement as the entry mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: Both streaks reaching their thresholds simultaneously is rare, so trade count may be low and Sharpe estimates noisy/overfit across WFO folds. Worse, the divergence may resolve as continuation rather than reversion - persistent up-gaps with red bodies can simply mean an uptrend opening strong and consolidating, in which case the short bleeds.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Autocorrelation regime flips streak polarity: ride up-streaks in momentum regime, buy down-streaks in reversion regime, 200-MA gated.
idea.hypothesis: A bull market (price above its 200-day MA) alternates between two micro-regimes: a trending regime where short-term returns are positively autocorrelated and up-close streaks tend to continue, and a choppy regime where returns are negatively autocorrelated and down-close streaks tend to snap back. A single fixed streak rule loses money in whichever regime it is mismatched to; conditioning the streak's polarity on the sign of rolling lag-1 return autocorrelation lets the same primitive harvest both.
idea.novelty_justification: Every streak idea in the list watches one fixed streak (up-close streak, days-above-MA streak, higher-lows streak, down-gap streak) with one fixed hypothesis. This strategy is genuinely regime-switching: a return-autocorrelation classifier flips WHICH streak is the entry trigger and inverts the entire entry/exit logic - momentum-continuation mode counts up-streaks, mean-reversion mode counts down-streaks. The predator-prey autocorrelation idea fades a z-score (different primitive) and only ever reverts; it never switches into a momentum mode. No prior idea swaps streak polarity via an autocorrelation regime.
idea.failure_mode: Rolling lag-1 autocorrelation is a noisy, lagging regime estimator. By the time it confidently labels a regime, the micro-regime may already have rotated, so the strategy systematically enters momentum trades into nascent reversion regimes (and vice versa), turning the switch into a coin flip plus costs.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Long when the percentile-rank velocity of distance-from-MA z-score (shockwave front speed) enters its own high percentile band.
idea.hypothesis: Distance-from-MA z-score can be treated as a 'density' coordinate; how fast its empirical percentile rank traverses that space is a kinematic shockwave front speed. When a momentum congestion clears, the front speed surges and tends to persist for a 1-2 week free-flow phase before a reverse (downstream) front forms. Trading the front speed rather than the z-score level captures the transition, not the static extreme.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas use the z-score level, its autocorrelation regime, seasonal climatology, or load-and-fire spring recoil. This trades the velocity of the empirical percentile rank of the z-score (a traffic-shockwave front speed) and gates it with a percentile threshold of that very velocity - a percentile-of-percentile-velocity mechanism absent from the list. The signal-reversal exit fires when the inverse condition (a downward front) forms, not on a stop or target.
idea.failure_mode: Percentile-rank velocity is a doubly-differenced, doubly-ranked quantity and is therefore very noisy; its high-band crossings may carry no real persistence, so entries can land precisely on short-term momentum exhaustion and the signal-reversal exit then bleeds via whipsaw round-trips.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade the SI epidemic-curve inflection: enter on the sign-flip of logistic-squashed volatility velocity, regime-gated, fixed-bar exit.
idea.hypothesis: Realized volatility behaves like an SI epidemic: a logistic-squashed vol z-score is an 'infected fraction' whose first difference is the new-infections-per-bar curve, which peaks at the curve's inflection. When new infections roll over inside a high-infection regime, the volatility shock has stopped accelerating and price is near a capitulation bottom; when they turn up inside a low-infection (calm) regime, complacency is ending and a drawdown is incipient. Trading the velocity sign-flip, rather than the vol level, times the regime transition itself.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing list items trade vol levels, half-of-peak ATR decay, climatological norms, or compression breakouts. This strategy's primitive is the second-order behavior of an SI logistic curve: it triggers on the zero-crossing of dI (new-infections velocity) of a logistic-squashed vol z-score, gated by which side of the I=0.5 epidemic inflection the market sits on. No list item uses the new-cases velocity sign-flip, the logistic SI squash, the susceptible/infected regime gate, or inverse-vol position sizing with a pure fixed-bar exit.
idea.failure_mode: The new-infections velocity (a differenced logistic of a vol z-score) whipsaws around the inflection, producing frequent low-conviction entries; with a fixed-bar exit and no trend filter every whipsaw pays commission plus slippage, and the short side additionally fights SPY's structural upward drift.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Long on ROC-acceleration zero-cross, volatility-targeted size scaled by acceleration strength, breakeven-then-ATR-trail exit
idea.hypothesis: The second derivative of price momentum (rate-of-change acceleration) inflects upward before the first derivative does, marking the point where selling decelerates and buyers regain control. Entering at that inflection, sizing inversely to realized volatility so each trade carries equal risk, and amplifying size when the acceleration impulse is strong, captures short multi-day continuation bursts while the breakeven-then-trail stop locks in the burst before mean reversion erases it.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior ROC-acceleration ideas in the list gate the trigger with a down-gap, a 200-MA filter, or a range-fill level and exit on signal reversal or fixed bars. This strategy uses the bare accel zero-cross as the trigger and makes volatility targeting the core position-sizing engine: size = clip(target_vol/realized_vol) multiplied by a normalized acceleration-strength factor. No other entry in the list combines an explicit annualized-vol-targeting size with an acceleration-magnitude scaling and a breakeven-then-trail exit — the mechanism is risk-parity sizing driven by impulse strength, not a new indicator gate.
idea.failure_mode: ROC acceleration is a noisy second difference; zero-crosses fire frequently in choppy ranges, producing many small whipsaw entries that the ATR trailing stop exits at a loss before the 3-5 day horizon develops, so commission and slippage drag dominate the modest continuation edge.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Finite-capacity queue of down-return shocks; buy when the saturated buffer drains after overflow in a 200-MA uptrend
idea.hypothesis: Selloffs during an uptrend behave like a queue: down-day return shocks 'arrive' and accumulate while up-day returns 'service' the backlog. The buffer has a finite capacity - once it overflows, sellers are saturated and incremental bad news no longer adds to the backlog. The exploitable inefficiency is the recovery handoff: when the overflowed buffer visibly drains while the trend regime remains bullish, panic supply has been absorbed and a 1-2 week mean-reversion bounce tends to follow.
idea.novelty_justification: No prior idea models close-to-close returns as a finite-capacity clamped queue with an explicit overflow/saturation event. The reservoir idea uses unbounded cumulative overnight-gap momentum and percentile bands; leaky-integrator ideas integrate range-percentile compression. Here the mechanism is an M/M/1-style arrival/service buffer hard-clamped to a capacity ceiling, where the trade trigger is the transition from a recorded overflow event to a measured drain below a low-water mark - a queueing-saturation construct absent from every listed idea.
idea.failure_mode: The capacity parameter is denominated in raw return units, so its 'right' value drifts with the volatility regime. A capacity calibrated on calm data rarely overflows when markets are quiet and overflows constantly when they are turbulent, meaning the saturation event degenerates into a plain volatility proxy and the entry collapses into an ordinary noisy buy-the-dip with no real edge from the queue construct.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Gap follow-through regime decides whether to long up-gaps or fade down-gaps; conviction-scaled size, ATR rolling-high trailing stop
idea.hypothesis: Whether an overnight gap continues or reverses intraday is itself a slow-moving regime: in some periods opening gaps get extended by the close, in others they get faded. By measuring the rolling agreement between gap direction and same-day open-to-close direction, the strategy detects which regime is active and longs the gap polarity that historically paid off in that regime. The inefficiency exploited is regime persistence in gap-response behavior that most gap strategies treat as static.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas trade gaps via fill/retention, streaks, reservoirs, weekday climatology, or treat gaps as infection events. The autocorrelation-regime idea in the list flips polarity using close-to-close return streaks. This strategy's regime variable is a gap-specific quantity nowhere else used: the rolling sign-agreement between the overnight gap and the SAME bar's intraday open-to-close move. That agreement metric (not return autocorrelation, not gap streaks) determines whether an up-gap or a down-gap is the long trigger, so the entry primitive itself switches sign with the regime.
idea.failure_mode: The gap-vs-intraday agreement metric is noisy and weakly persistent; the regime label lags the actual regime, so the strategy can switch to fading down-gaps just as the market enters a genuine downtrend, turning the long-only fade entries into knife-catching with a trailing stop that only ratchets up from a sinking entry.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade gaps in the direction of the gap only when the gap day's intraday range stays compressed (accepted repricing)
idea.hypothesis: A gap that occurs on a session whose intraday true range stays unusually compressed reflects genuine repricing that liquidity providers are not contesting - there is no two-sided range expansion fighting the move, so the market has quietly accepted the new level. Such 'quiet' gaps tend to follow through over the next one to two days, whereas gaps accompanied by wide-range sessions are contested and mean-revert. Range expansion behaves like a predator that hunts and erases gaps; when that predator is absent, the gap survives.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas confirm gaps with volume, close-retention, weekday climatology, gap streaks, or a cumulative gap reservoir - all of which want MORE activity to validate the gap. This strategy inverts that: it requires the ABSENCE of range expansion (intraday true range below its rolling-median baseline) as the confirming primitive. The two-primitive AND is gap-sign AND range-compression, a mechanism where silence rather than confirmation validates the signal. No prior idea uses range compression on the gap bar itself as a continuation filter.
idea.failure_mode: The premise that a compressed-range gap day signals accepted repricing may simply be wrong - a quiet gap day can be low-liquidity drift that reverts, and most economically meaningful gaps (earnings, macro prints) arrive WITH range expansion, so the compression filter may systematically select small, informationless gaps that carry no follow-through edge and just bleed costs.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy deep multi-week declines only when the sell-off shockwave decelerates (recent drop slower than prior drop)
idea.hypothesis: A sustained multi-week price decline overshoots fair value because selling propagates like a traffic jam: a shockwave front sweeping through the order book. The decline reverts not when the cumulative drop is deepest, but when the front decelerates - when the most recent leg of selling is materially slower than the leg before it. Pairing a deep-discount condition with a measured deceleration of return velocity isolates exhausted sell-offs from still-accelerating crashes.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing drawdown ideas use increment autocorrelation turning anti-persistent, z-score recoil velocity, or staircase support. This strategy measures the second difference of cumulative close-to-close return: it compares the summed return of the recent window against the summed return of the immediately prior window and requires the front to have slowed (recent leg less negative than prior leg, prior leg genuinely negative). It is a deceleration-of-velocity gate on raw return sums, not an autocorrelation, z-score, or channel construct.
idea.failure_mode: In a genuine bear market the shockwave decelerates repeatedly during bear-market rallies, firing entries that get run over by the next acceleration leg; the breakeven-then-trail exit caps losses but a string of -2 ATR stops in a persistent downtrend will bleed the strategy faster than the 3-4 week winners recover.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when SNR-filtered ROC acceleration shows upward convexity in a 200-MA uptrend; fixed-bar exit
idea.hypothesis: When an existing positive trend's rate-of-change is itself speeding up (positive convexity), it reflects fresh demand entering rather than a tiring move, and such momentum tends to persist over the following few weeks. Most acceleration readings are noise; gating on a signal-to-noise ratio isolates the rare bars where the convexity is statistically meaningful. Filtering to bars above the 200-day MA keeps the convexity bet aligned with the structural regime where momentum continuation is most reliable.
idea.novelty_justification: The existing ROC-acceleration entry triggers on a zero-cross (acceleration just turning positive) with a breakeven-trail exit. This one is the opposite signature: it requires ROC to be ALREADY positive AND acceleration to be strongly positive (upward convexity of an in-progress trend), and it never trades the inflection-from-decline cases other entries chase. The trigger is a noise-normalized SNR threshold on acceleration, not a sign change, and the exit is a pure fixed-bar clock with no path-dependent trailing. No other idea combines convex-momentum continuation, SNR-gated acceleration, and a fixed-bar horizon.
idea.failure_mode: Acceleration is a second derivative and is extremely noisy; even after SNR normalization, the high-convexity bars may cluster near short-term local tops (blow-off bursts), so a rigid fixed-bar hold could systematically exit into the mean-reversion that follows an exhausted accelerating move.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Buy failed downside breakouts: bar undercuts the N-day low but closes back in its upper range
idea.hypothesis: When price probes below a multi-week low intraday but the close recovers into the upper portion of that same bar's range, the new low was a liquidity flush that got absorbed by buyers. Stops were swept and supply exhausted, so the close-to-close trajectory tends to revert upward over the next several days. Requiring both range primitives to agree filters out genuine breakdowns where the close stays pinned at the low.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing range ideas use channel position, range compression percentiles, leaky-integrator coils, or closes pinning near the low for exhaustion. This is the opposite construction: it requires an active downside RANGE EXTENSION (a fresh new-low probe) combined with intrabar REJECTION (close in the bar's upper range). It is a failed-breakout / Wyckoff-spring mechanism, not a compression-then-buy-the-channel-node mechanism. No listed idea pairs a new-low undercut with same-bar close-location recovery; the gap-rejection idea uses overnight gaps, not the high-low range itself.
idea.failure_mode: In a sustained bear leg every day prints a lower low, and many such bars also close mid-range on a dead-cat intraday bounce; the strategy then buys repeatedly into a falling knife, and the fixed-bar exit with no signal-based stop forces it to ride each loss for the full holding window.

slots.exit_rule: signal-reversal exit (exit only when the entry condition flips)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when close sits low in an asymmetric upside/downside semi-deviation volatility corridor during downside-vol dominance; mirror-position exit.
idea.hypothesis: When recent downside return semi-deviation exceeds upside semi-deviation, sellers have temporarily widened the lower 'rail' of price's volatility corridor, pushing the close into a relatively low position that overstates how far price has actually traveled. Such fear-driven displacements tend to be partially retraced within a day or two as the corridor's neutral point (which itself shifts with the up/down vol skew) snaps back. Buying a low relative position only while downside vol dominates isolates panic dislocations from healthy trend pullbacks.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the symmetric 'k ATRs below median' idea or z-score distance ideas, this corridor is asymmetric: its upper half-width uses upside return semi-deviation and its lower half-width uses downside semi-deviation, so the neutral relative-position level itself moves with fear/greed (rp at the midline equals dn_dev/(up_dev+dn_dev)). It is not a Donchian/channel-fill mechanism (no raw high-low) and not a velocity/shockwave-speed measure. The signal is the close's fractional position inside a volatility-shaped geometry, gated by a downside-vs-upside semi-deviation skew regime — a mechanism absent from the list.
idea.failure_mode: In sustained downtrends downside-vol dominance persists and the close stays pinned low in the corridor, so the strategy re-enters long repeatedly while the mirror exit at band_hi rarely fires — long-only knife-catching with no protective stop, bleeding through extended bear phases.

slots.exit_rule: breakeven-then-trail (after price reaches +X%, move the stop to entry price, then trail by k*ATR; the stop only ever moves up, never down)

idea.one_line_summary: Ride trends while the prevalence of volatility-significant directional bars (infection rate) is rising and unsaturated
idea.hypothesis: Strong trends are sustained when a growing fraction of recent bars produce moves that are large relative to volatility, in one consistent direction - a contagion of directional participation analogous to an SI epidemic curve. While that participation is still spreading (rising) and has not yet saturated the susceptible pool, the trend still has fuel. Entering during the growth phase of this infection prevalence captures the strongest segment of the move before exhaustion sets in.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the SI inflection idea (#22), which keys off the sign-flip of a logistic-squashed volatility velocity, this strategy builds an explicit prevalence statistic: the rolling fraction of bars whose return exceeds c*sigma in each direction. The entry mechanism is a three-part epidemic condition - net prevalence above threshold, prevalence still rising (dI/dt>0), and below a saturation cap - which is mechanically distinct from streak-polarity (autocorrelation idea), draining-buffer (shock-queue idea), and displacement-speed (shockwave idea) approaches. No listed idea counts volatility-significant directional bars as an infection prevalence gated by an anti-saturation veto.
idea.failure_mode: The infection prevalence is a slow rolling count, so it lags price. By the time net prevalence crosses the threshold while still rising, the trend may already be mature; the saturation cap is a blunt filter and may admit late entries, producing buy-high/sell-low whipsaws when a choppy regime keeps prevalence oscillating near the threshold.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when ROC acceleration breaches its own noise band (yield strength); elastic sub-band moves ignored, fixed ATR vol-stop.
idea.hypothesis: Most bar-to-bar changes in trend speed are elastic noise that springs back, but once trend acceleration exceeds the recent dispersion of acceleration itself it represents a genuine, plastic regime shift where the uptrend has permanently deformed to a faster state. Entering only on these supra-threshold acceleration events captures real trend strengthening while filtering the elastic chop that traps zero-cross or convexity systems.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ROC-acceleration ideas trade the zero-cross (sign flip), upward convexity, or pair acceleration with range-fill. This strategy never looks at sign-crossing or convexity: it compares acceleration magnitude against a self-referential yield-strength threshold (the rolling std of acceleration). The trigger is an amplitude breach of a noise band, an explicit elastic-vs-plastic deformation gate, not a directional crossing — a different mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: ROC acceleration is a noisy near-second-order quantity that frequently spikes hardest right as a trend exhausts; entries can cluster at local peaks, so the fixed vol-stop is hit shortly after entry and the strategy buys blow-off tops.

slots.exit_rule: rolling-high trailing stop (track the highest close since entry; exit when close falls k*ATR below that in-trade high-water mark; the stop only ratchets up)

idea.one_line_summary: Count consecutive below-median-range bars; on the expansion bar that ends the quiet streak, trade ROC-acceleration direction.
idea.hypothesis: Volatility arrives in clusters separated by quiet 'slack tide' lulls where True Range stays below its rolling median. The duration of a lull, not its depth, predicts that a directional move is being stored; when the lull breaks with an expansion bar, the sign of recent rate-of-change acceleration reveals which way the stored move discharges. Trading that release in the direction of the 200-day trend captures the discharge before it fully prices in.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing range-compression ideas trigger on a continuous tension integrator crossing a threshold (loaded-spring), on a price channel breakout (Donchian/staircase ceiling), or on mean-reversion to a channel node (neap-tide). This strategy uses neither magnitude nor price level: the trigger is a discrete COUNT of consecutive below-median-True-Range bars and the specific bar that ENDS that streak via range expansion. Compression is measured as event duration; direction is a separate decision delegated to ROC acceleration sign at the release bar. No listed idea uses quiet-streak duration as the firing primitive.
idea.failure_mode: ROC acceleration sign at a single release bar is a noisy, low-information direction estimate; in choppy markets quiet streaks end constantly with no follow-through, so the strategy fires frequently into whipsaws and the trailing stop bleeds commission and slippage faster than the occasional real discharge pays for it.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade the sign duty-cycle of distance-from-MA z-score; long/short on trend-strength threshold cross, conviction-scaled size, profit-target/time-stop exit.
idea.hypothesis: Trend strength is better measured by persistence than by magnitude: in a genuine trend, price spends a lopsided fraction of recent bars on one side of its moving average, regardless of how stretched any single bar is. The fraction of recent bars with a positive distance-from-MA z-score (the 'duty cycle' of the standing wave around the MA node) crossing a high skew threshold marks a trend that has just become statistically established, and such trends carry a 1-2 day continuation edge before the standing wave rebalances toward its node.
idea.novelty_justification: No listed idea uses the time-fraction (duty cycle) of the z-score sign as the trade trigger. The closest neighbors trade z-score magnitude/velocity (shockwave speed, elastic recoil, climatology de-seasonalization), bar counts of a different test (infection rate of volatility-significant bars), or streaks of consecutive same-sign bars. Duty cycle is distinct from a streak: it tolerates interruptions and measures occupancy, not run length. The mechanism is occupancy of one antinode side of the MA node, fired on its threshold cross.
idea.failure_mode: Duty-cycle confirmation lags: by the time the fraction-above-MA crosses a high threshold, the trend is already mature, so the +X% profit target rarely triggers within 2 bars and most trades exit via the time-stop near breakeven, where the 7bps round-trip cost turns a flat expectancy slightly negative.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Low-vol anomaly: long when realized-volatility percentile rank stays in its bottom band two bars; vol-targeted size, fixed ATR vol-stop.
idea.hypothesis: Periods of compressed realized volatility (volatility ranking low in its own trailing distribution) tend to precede positive drift, because the predator-prey balance between volatility and price favors the prey when the predator population is depleted. The market's low-volatility anomaly persists because risk-averse capital underweights quiet uptrends. Confirming the compression over two bars filters single-bar noise that would otherwise trigger on transient quiet days inside a turbulent regime.
idea.novelty_justification: The list contains range-compression and spring/coil ideas built on price-range percentiles or distance-from-MA z-scores. This strategy ranks realized RETURN volatility itself (close-to-close std) as the entry primitive, exploiting the low-volatility anomaly, and uses that same volatility to inverse-scale position size (true volatility-targeting). No listed idea combines a percentile rank of realized vol as the trigger with vol-targeted sizing; the mechanism is the vol distribution, not the price-range or MA-distance distribution.
idea.failure_mode: Low realized volatility frequently sits at major market tops just before a regime break; the strategy will buy quiet uptrends and get repeatedly stopped out by k*ATR gaps when compression resolves downward, since calm precedes crashes as often as it precedes drift.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)
slots.constraint_twist: percentile threshold instead of fixed level

idea.one_line_summary: Overnight-vs-intraday volatility partition decides whether to follow or fade the latest gap; fixed ATR vol-stop.
idea.hypothesis: When overnight gap volatility dominates intraday volatility, price-moving information is arriving while markets are closed, so the most recent gap is informative and tends to persist. When intraday volatility dominates, gaps are mostly noise injected by thin pre-market liquidity and tend to reverse. Splitting realized volatility into its overnight and intraday components therefore reveals which gap-trading behavior is currently profitable.
idea.novelty_justification: Prior gap strategies key off the gap's own magnitude, direction, weekday climatology, intraday range compression, or observed gap follow-through. This one ignores all of those and instead decomposes total volatility into two structural buckets (close-to-open vs open-to-close), and the percentile rank of their ratio is the regime switch that flips between gap-following and gap-fading. The deciding variable is a volatility-source partition, not any property of the gap itself.
idea.failure_mode: The overnight/intraday volatility ratio may not be a stable regime indicator - it can be dragged around by a handful of outlier sessions, leaving the gap-follow vs gap-fade edge too weak to survive 2bps commission plus 5bps slippage on frequent 2-day round trips.

slots.exit_rule: profit-target + time-stop (exit at +X% gain or after N bars, whichever comes first)

idea.one_line_summary: Trade close-direction streaks only when they terminate on a turn-of-week weekday, betting on the weekend-effect reversal
idea.hypothesis: The weekend effect concentrates equity weakness around Mondays and the run-up into Fridays. A multi-day down streak that culminates on a Monday has likely just exhausted the seasonally weak window, so it tends to rebound; a multi-day up streak culminating on a Friday faces the seasonally weak weekend window and tends to pull back. The weekday on which a streak ends — not the streak length alone — carries the predictive information.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing ideas use weekday gap climatology (overnight gaps ranked against a weekday's history) or flip streak polarity by a measured autocorrelation regime. This strategy keeps a plain daily close-direction streak but gates its interpretation purely by the calendar weekday on which the streak terminates: a down-streak ending Monday is a long, an up-streak ending Friday is a short. The seasonality is a discrete weekday gate on streak termination, not a continuous climatology rank nor a statistically-estimated regime — a different mechanism.
idea.failure_mode: The weekend/Monday effect has decayed sharply and partially reversed in liquid index ETFs since the 1990s. Requiring a streak to end on exactly one weekday makes qualifying entries rare, so the WFO test windows may contain too few trades for the edge to be distinguishable from noise.

slots.exit_rule: fixed-bar exit (exit exactly N bars after entry, no signal-based exit)

idea.one_line_summary: Long when prevalence of upward range-translation bars (HH+HL) crosses a critical infection threshold in a 200-MA uptrend; fixed-bar exit
idea.hypothesis: When a trend is genuine, the entire high-low range repeatedly translates upward (higher high AND higher low together), not just the close. Treating each such range-translation bar as an 'infected' case, the rolling infected fraction behaves like an SI epidemic curve: once prevalence breaches a critical threshold the move has become self-sustaining and tends to continue for several days. Traders underreact to this structural shift because they watch closes, not full-range geometry.
idea.novelty_justification: The signal primitive is pure range geometry: a binary event defined solely by whether the whole [low,high] interval shifts up (HH and HL simultaneously). No prior idea uses range-translation events. The closest, 'prevalence of volatility-significant directional bars', keys on close direction plus volatility significance and rides while it is 'rising and unsaturated'; this instead counts a range-only translation event and triggers on a single fresh threshold cross with a mechanical fixed-bar exit. It is not a Donchian breakout, not a streak count, and not close- or ATR-based.
idea.failure_mode: The prevalence-threshold cross may fire late — by the time enough range-translation bars accumulate, the bulk of the multi-day move is already spent, so the fixed 3-5 bar hold buys the exhaustion tail and gives back gains on the inevitable mean reversion.

slots.exit_rule: fixed volatility-stop (exit when close falls below entry price minus k*ATR - fixed, not trailing)

idea.one_line_summary: Constructive interference: enter long when overnight gap AND intraday body both push up; fixed ATR vol-stop
idea.hypothesis: A trading day can be split into two return half-cycles - the overnight gap (close-to-open) and the intraday session (open-to-close). When both half-cycles are decisively up on the same bar, the two waves are in phase: institutional repositioning overnight is being confirmed and extended by intraday demand rather than faded. This rare phase-alignment marks an antinode of upward energy that tends to carry over a multi-week horizon, whereas a strong gap met by an intraday fade is destructive interference and reverts.
idea.novelty_justification: Existing gap ideas use percentile climatology, cumulative gap reservoirs, gap-day range compression, or overnight-vs-intraday volatility partitioning to follow/fade. This is none of those: it is a same-bar AND of two directional return components (gap sign and intraday body sign), each gated by an ATR-scaled magnitude threshold. The divergence idea in the list shorts when overnight and intraday streaks disagree; this trades the opposite event - their instantaneous agreement on a single bar - and is not streak-based. The mechanism is phase alignment of two return half-cycles, not momentum accumulation or compression.
idea.failure_mode: An up-gap that continues intraday is itself a strong-momentum bar; buying it near a local high means entering at elevated price, so a routine pullback can hit the fixed k*ATR stop within days and the multi-week thesis never gets a chance to play out, producing many small whipsaw losses.

idea.one_line_summary: Trade runaway (unfilled) gaps only when the 2-week ROC AND-agrees with the gap direction; breakeven-then-trail exit.
idea.hypothesis: A gap that the market refuses to fill intraday is a propagating shockwave: the repricing was accepted, not rejected, so order flow tends to extend it over the following 1-2 weeks. Most index gaps get partially or fully filled (dissipating shockwaves) and carry no edge; the rare runaway gap, when it also points the same way as the prevailing multi-week drift, marks genuine directional pressure rather than noise.
idea.novelty_justification: Unlike the cumulative gap reservoir, weekday gap climatology, gap streak, or follow-through-regime ideas, this strategy never aggregates or classifies past gap behavior. Its entry primitive is the intrabar fill geometry of a single gap (how little of the gap the low/high retraced), which no prior idea uses. The two-primitive AND pairs a fast single-bar event (a runaway gap) with a slow co-equal directional primitive (the 10-day ROC) — distinct from 'constructive interference' which ANDs two same-day intraday signals, and distinct from 'compressed-range gap day' which gates on intraday range rather than gap-fill containment.
idea.failure_mode: On SPY the unfilled-down-gap short side is the weak link: index down-gaps are habitually bought back over subsequent weeks, so shorts likely bleed even with a trend filter, and the breakeven-then-trail stop may whipsaw the position flat on a single noisy bar before the intended 1-2 week move matures.
